what_happened: Configured actions/dependency-review-action@v4 to use an external config file for vulnerability exemptions
why: The action's `config-file` parameter accepts a path to a YAML file that can contain all action configuration (including fail-on-severity and allow-ghsas), enabling a single authoritative source for both CI config and the audit trail of approved exemptions
fix_applied: Created .github/vulnerability-exemptions.yml with fail-on-severity, allow-ghsas (machine-enforced), and an exemptions audit list (human-readable). Moved the inline fail-on-severity from pr-test.yml into the config file so config-file is the sole source of truth.
pattern_to_remember: For actions/dependency-review-action@v4, use `config-file` to point at a YAML file — this replaces ALL inline `with:` settings. The config file key names match the action's input names exactly (e.g. fail-on-severity, allow-ghsas). The `allow-ghsas` list in the config file is what the action actually enforces; a separate human-readable `exemptions` block can document CVE IDs, justification, expiry_date, and approver for the audit trail.
